key_insight: High-velocity air-fuel spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This enables adherent NiTi functional coatings for wear, friction, corrosion, and thermal-actuation applications on steel components.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi coatings onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Assessed actuation and shape-memory behavior via 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests
evidence_signal: The coatings were 100–300 μm thick and, despite defects and inhomogeneity, showed martensitic transformation plus thermal actuation and shape-memory effects after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings have limited tensile strength and significant defects, so post-spray annealing and adhesion/defect control are critical for implementation.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
